given: a warehouse operator with no stock recorded at a location
when: the home screen is opened for that location
then: an explicit "No stock at this location" message is shown in place of the table, never a blank page

independence.rationale: AC1 and AC2 cover the populated table and its quantity alignment; this adds the distinct empty-state outcome shown when no records exist, which can fail (blank page) while the populated-table behavior stays green, and vice versa.

architectural_notes: E2E: the empty-result branch of the AC1 read path. The stock-repository returns zero rows for the location, the stock-service returns an empty collection (not an error), and the React SPA renders the explicit "No stock at this location" message in place of the table. Owner: app/services returns the empty listing distinctly from a populated one; the client owns the empty-state view. Cross-cutting: realizes the PO's clean-render preference (never a blank region / null crash) as NFR-F1-empty-state-clean-render. Verified against the real branch with no seeded rows for the location, asserting the message, not a blank page.
